review_title: Delivering insightful feedback with Culture Amp.
description: We've been having a challenge tracking the employee goals and progress, and also updating them respectively which affected their overall evaluation. With Culture Amp, it is now much easier to track the employee performance and give credit where due. It has enabled us to gain valuable insights into employees' experiences, identify areas for improvement, and make data-driven decisions to enhance their company culture and overall performance and productivity. Overall, Culture Amp is an Excellent and easy to integrate software for any organization with intent of improving their employees performances.
author: Frankline K.
pros: This is one of the most user friendly software that integrates top quality analytics to gain insights onto the employee performances while ensuring that the user interface is easy to use and is available to employees at all levels in the organization.
cons: I feel the 1-on-1s feature should be upgraded in that, it should be able to send an email of responses whenever a direct report or manager updates the conversation or shares a reply. Other than that, Culture Amp is the best.

description: My company purchased the survey module from Culture Amp last year. We looked at 10 or so other vendors, 2 of them making it to the final round, and Culture Amp being our final choice due to the experience we had during the sales process, the great UI, and and the support offered by their company. We've been happy with the tool after running one large company-wide survey and a couple of smaller ones. Culture Amp works hard to make sure employee data is kept confidential.
author: Zach H.
pros: Lovely UI, easy to manage employee data, configure surveys, and track results through the tool.
cons: There is very little to speak negatively about Culture Amp. The application is great and support has met expectations.

review_title: Culture Amp is great with people sciences, but the tool itself is terrible
description: Not helpful with more strategic initiatives.
author: Patty K.
pros: Not good for large scale and high growth organizations. Their integration with Rippling is a joke and leads to terrible data and duplicative profiles. Very buggy -- when you run performance cycles the UX is different for self reflection vs performance. It means you have to manually update and edit everything. Also, they don't have on demand customer service -- just the chat bot, so if you need anything more complex.. like talent grids or ways to review calibration modules, you have to contact your CS team and in our experience their solutions are super limited. Mostly they tell you to just download the CSV and then manually create reporting or detailed info outside of the system. Not great.
cons: UX is terrible -- it is not user friendly at all.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love that Culture Amp just makes things so simple for the People team now. They have survey templates readily available so we don't even have to design the surveys anymore. It's very user friendly for employees to take the survey and the reporting is the best part. It really protects anonymity while allowing you to take targeted action.
cons: While the Performance features have many of the things you would expect in an OKR software, it's not as strong as their survey module yet. For example, there are opportunities to improve in the features for adding and tracking OKR progress. This is a newer feature for them though so I am hopeful to see where they will be at in a few years from now.

review_title: Best tool to measure employee engagement & performance management
description: Has been very good, however a bit expensive
author: Yitzhak Vini S.
pros: Ease of use & the analytics of the engagement survey is very good
cons: Poor integration with google calendar & inability to send reminders in performance reviews

review_title: Not for people with learning difficulties, good software but awful product support
description: My experience has been terrible, not due to the software but due to the Product Support team. They take hours to get back to you and it does feel like they are not concerned with smaller clients.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The software platform is robust. It has a lot of helpful features.
cons: Setting up the engagement survey can be painful, especially with awful product support. Most guides are in writing, so dyslexics will find get confused. They have a lot of bespoke terminology that takes time to get used to.

review_title: First time using, amazing so far
description: Overall the tool has been amazing from a user's perspective. I'm excited to continue to use especially now that our organization is asking for our opinions on returning to work in the office.
author: Gustavo O.
pros: Culture amp is a leader in its space when it comes to employee engagement and I can see why! At this moment our organization is only using culture amp for employee engagement. So far I've noticed that (1) it's easy to get out to your employees (2) the tool is very easy to use (3) it's very appealing and looks really nice (4) Great customer support (5) easy to use for both HR managers and users.
cons: I've heard from the HR team that at times it can be difficult to manage the system from the admin side, however, from a user's perspective - the tool has been amazing.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Really positive. The engagement/survey side has been most used by our business. We are still encouraging employees to use the 1-2-1 functions more as not everyone has adopted them.
author: Emily R.
pros: The fact it summaries answers from employee engagement surveys into themes (this has saved us lots of time!)
cons: Doesn't always sync perfectly to our employee list, so we are reminding people about surveys on our intranet and very occasionally a new starter hasn't been included (as lists haven't synced)

review_title: A great tool to drive performance and engagement
description: Very positive. Our employees are much more active in our surveys now that Culture Amp is processing all of the responses. It's also much easier to analyze results when you're not running it through a google form.
author: Jeremy C.
pros: Culture Amp makes it easy to set up performance management and employee engagement activities. Their customer service is quick to respond to any questions we might have. The ease of use is fantastic.
cons: Confidentiality protections are tough to undo if you set them up wrong. Definitely make sure you read up on them before releasing a survey!

description: I would definitely recommend using Culture Amp to any company over the size of 50 (I am not sure that the investment would be worth it for smaller organisations), as it really helps shaping and monitoring the most important element of any organisation: culture (it's in the name, after all!)
author: Valentino F.
pros: Culture Amp has a bit of everything that contributes to build a company culture. From surveys to performance, from engagement to 1:1s, to the ever-important feedback exchanges, the possibilities seem endless! The science to back up things like engagement survey, recognition and 360 feedback is solid, and that is what makes CA the best in class for me.
cons: The platform might not be super intuitive for non-expert users. The naming convention might need some getting used to (but the same can be said for other platforms), and the mechanics of how certain things work are sometimes a little intricate.

author: Kara K.
pros: It is easy to use and provide good insights
cons: The survey presentation can felt a little overwhelming

review_title: good concept, a bit of a bumpy ride without a clear view through the windshield
author: Richard W.
pros: I believe in Culture Amp's purpose of brining together functionality around employee engagement, goal setting, performance management and improving organizational culture into one comprehensive development zone. From a high level view, it seems to integrate the right components in a reasonably intuitive structure There is ample room for customization.
cons: However the features themselves turn out to feel rather clunky and disjointed. Perhaps if you can dedicate a brilliant team of visionaries and administrators to fully realize Culture Amp's potential, this can be overcome in your implementation. But I find the many limitations imposed by our configuration to make the application more frustrating than propulsive. Limitations include inscrutably opaque scheduling functions (first of all an administrator has to manage all the schedules, then the only way to look up when something should occur is to find the group of that functionality (say Performance>Self Assessments>Direct Reports Self-Assessments) then drill down into individual months (say May 2024) to see who is scheduled in that month - and only past and current months can be seen. So effectively you cannot see an annual schedule and you cannot see your own obligations in the same frame that you can see your direct reports' obligations. When completing such functions as performance reviews and self-assessments, there is an arcane methodology for submitting documents for review and sharing them, along with deadlines set by an administrator that close documents for completion or editing, and require adminsitrator interventions to enable sharing. All of these may serve some purpose but they really serve to make it seem hard, sometimes impossible, to do what you're supposed to do. And trying to understand what is working for you or against you to complete these tasks is difiicult. I feel like the developers may have had some brilliant ideas but then let themselves get so bogged down in controlling every detail that they don't see the oppressive cloud hovering over the product. A particular detail that I found very frustrating involved the in-app support features. There are several places you can seek explanations or instructions. Upon doing so, a little bot survey asks about your experience with the help and whether you have any feedback for Culture Amp on your issue. It then asks if Culture Amp can contact you about your feedback. On several occasions I wrote some very detailed observations and suggestions, and actually did hope that I would be able to talk to someone at the company about these. After a few times, I decided to send one of these responses in to the support team at Contact Us to see if I could get any traction. I explained that I had submitted these and similar details to the survey bot. First, the reps several times over tried to steer me into an explanation of why our site administrator, not Culture Amp staff would be the person to talk to about changing our survey questions. When I finally convinced someone familiar with the survey bot that I was not talking about any custom survey of our own, they explained that the survey bot responses are not captured with a link to any individual user who submits respoinses - they are only catured and analyzed in aggregate. I naturally suggested that in that case it might be prudent of them to remove the question about whether Culture Amp can contact us about that response, since they appear to have neither the intention nor the means. They disagreed and the issue sort of dissolved into the overall cloud of opacity under which I use, but don't reallu understand the inner workings of this application.

description: Have not faced any particular issues while using it
author: Tair K.
pros: Overall positive experience using Culture Amp for specific task
cons: Easy to use and customise for specific needs
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Culture Amp provides insights that move the culture needle
description: Insight into our culture, the specific experiences of our people, a view of our managers and people (strengths/areas for improvement), an easy-to-use exit process that allows us to compare results and view trends.
author: Jenny S.
pros: Two years ago when we were looking for an engagement survey tool, I came across Culture Amp in a StartUP HR forum. It was recommended over and over by different people at different companies so I looked into it, along with a number of other solutions. Culture Amp came out on top in every comparison I did for its features, ease-of-use, domain expertise, and pricing. After we launched our first survey and shared results with our leadership team, I knew we had made the right choice. The tool is easy-to-use as an administrator and elegant/simple for end users. There are useful new features being added regularly, and every product improvement or new offering comes with strong communications backed by good science/thought leadership, and clearly having taken customer feedback into consideration. We now use Culture Amp for: -Engagement surveys -Manager 180 surveys/feedback -Individual 360s -Exit Processes (and I expect this list to continue to grow) The customer service is excellent with a responsive team that talks to me like a real person. We have great conversations, they get me the answers I seek, and they always appreciate my input about how to make the offerings even better. Our leaders and employees find value in the tool, and as an HR team, our ability to deliver insightful results to individuals and teams makes us highly impactful to our company as we all work to continue to build our great culture.
cons: I find myself going to Culture Amp to do everything and everything it does not do, nor should it. I like it so much, however, that other options pale in comparison. For instance, we are developing a new performance feedback process - not a 360, not a survey, and I'd love to use Culture Amp for that purpose but it's not set up for that, especially because it isn't integrated with our HRIS. Each time we do a new, global survey, I need to upload our data to Culture Amp. It's not a bad process, but I'd love a more seamless option (it may be a limit of our HRIS vs. Culture Amp).

review_title: A great (but limited) tool for employee engagement
description: If you are looking for a no frills service for engagement survey, culture amp is fantastic!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We utilized this tool specifically for shipping out employee engagement surveys, and found it to be best in class for this service. Though we ultimately switched to Lattice, the engagement surveys and the features we had with the surveys were incredible. We were able to map questions so we never lost data when we developed a new question or adapted one that didn't have a perfect match in their system.
cons: We switched to lattice ultimately as we wanted a tool with more functionality. We use lattice for engagement surveys, career mapping, performance reviews, and managing career growth.

review_title: Fabulous platform that opens two-way of communication between employees and employer
author: Mohammad B.
pros: The best part of Culture Amp is the part of collecting data through surveys from employees that helps the business in fixing any issues that the company faces through the feedback. The integration with Slack helps a lot by notifying all the activities. The support system is very nice and there is a lot of training available that has helped us a lot to have hands on experience before using the platform
cons: I would say the flaw I see is the pricing structure of Culture Amp as for what it does, it is expensive. There should be new automation and integration with other third party platforms too.

description: Good experience on using the results of CultureAmp to take action on insights provided by the tool.
author: Luis C.
pros: It makes analysis of the responses evaluating emotions and qualitative and quantitative scores; the result of these evaluations and analytics allows a great analysis of different areas of the business.
cons: Just the fact that it is more expensive than other options but it provides much more features than other commercial options.

author: Aurelie M.
pros: it can be integrated with my HRMS It offers lots of templates
cons: They do not have probation reviews it can be somewhat difficult to extract results, but once you graps it it becomes very user friendly

author: Noor S.
pros: It's super easy to use for a 360 degree feedback cycle that we use at work twice a year. The functionality is simple and makes sense and we've not run into any issues where users are confused.
cons: It becomes difficult to track goals when they are more abstract in nature.

review_title: The BEST Tool I've Used In My Professional Career
description: Culture Amp is our tool and resource for our HR team that really allows us to dive into the team member journey -- from candidate surveys to onboarding and exit interviews - and everything in between. We've learned so much about our staff. I also love all of the resources within the Culture Amp trainings.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Culture Amp has endless resources to support your organization. I love the reporting features because it really lets you drill down into how your team members are feeling, how they are engaged, what's working, what's not, etc. It's also extremely easy to manage and track access to your organization's report. Finally - the customer service is second to none.
cons: Culture Amp is on the pricey side. While we currently use it for surveys, there are other products we'd like to try - they're just out of budget at this time. (I still believe it is worth the price though - you get what you pay for).

description: Where are a lot of different outlets on the culture camp
author: Ruben G.
pros: Just experience going through the comments and different experiences
cons: Good comments throughout its entire culture what did youJust experience going through the comments and different experiences
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Pretty easy to access and very self-explanatory. I am never lost, everything you need is easily accessible in my opinion.
author: Nicolette N.
pros: I find the onboard, employee training very important and informative. I also Find it to be easy when rating a new Employee hire Canidate. It's easy to review the feedback of other Team members and make the process easy.
cons: Its Easy for the most part....I have not had any issues.

author: Pedro A.
pros: Culture amp really helps me evaluating a multinacional team, with people located in 5 countries using the same platform. Easy to setup goals and tracking them, setup 1 on 1 and evaluate the performance of my team is much more simple than before
cons: A translation tool could be important to incorporate for people that are not confortable in the English language

description: different component processes (i.e., planning, monitoring, developing, rating, rewarding, lessening) work together and support each other, resulting in natural, effective performance management. my overall experience we need to just set a SMART goal: it's specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and timely into Culture Amp.
author: sultana N.
pros: Adopt a culture of evidence-based decision-making.
cons: To align individual and organizational objectives provide clear idea.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A Great Way to Gauge Employee Experience
description: Overall I have had a great experience with it. The UI is very intuitive and I love that we are able to get a sense for how employees feel about certain aspects. It allows us a greater view into what we need to improve as an organization.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: We recently implemented Culture Amp at our organization and I love it! We have been primarily using it for employee engagement surveys. I love that you have the chance to send surveys that are anonymous for employees and also surveys that are tied to that person specifically. I also like how we can see when the survey was sent to them, and send them a reminder if they haven't completed it yet. We used it most recently for our global benefits survey and it was very enlightening in terms of what employees would like to see added to our plan vs what benefits they were the least interested in.
cons: We have had a great experience with it so far. The only thing I would like to see added is a way for employees to elaborate on why they choose "strongly agree" vs "agree."
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
